title: Data contracts for the global SFC from-whom-to-whom matrix
grounding_rule: Each contract was grounded against its publisher via live fetch; facts the publisher did not confirm are listed per-contract under unverified[]. Nothing filled from memory.

contracts.role_in_matrix: Cross-border bank claims (financial assets) and liabilities by counterparty country and counterparty sector, with currency breakdown; supplies bilateral bank positions (reporting-country vs counterparty-country cells) for the from-whom-to-whom matrix, on a residence basis.

marginals supplier (secondary/derived) — provides country-level external IIP positions (gross external assets and liabilities, and net IIP) usable as row/column control totals/marginals for the FWTW matrix; NOT a from-whom-to-whom (bilateral counterpart) cell source. Note Brookings is a think tank re-publishing a derived database built largely on IMF/national-source data, not a primary statistical agency.

Euro-area and EU member-state sectoral balance sheets (stocks/positions) and transactions, including who-to-whom (counterpart-sector) financial accounts, supplying cells and marginals for the euro-area block of the global from-whom-to-whom matrix on a residency basis (ESA 2010).

contracts.publisher: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
contracts.role_in_matrix: US sectoral marginals (sector-by-instrument transactions F.* and levels L.* matrices, balance sheets B.*), SNA-aligned Integrated Macroeconomic Accounts (IMA, S.1-S.9), and estimated from-whom-to-whom (Issuer-to-Holder) bilateral sector-by-instrument cells from the Enhanced Financial Accounts.

Supplies the currency-composition marginal of official reserve assets (USD/EUR/JPY/GBP/CNY/CHF/AUD/CAD/Other shares and USD nominal values) at the World aggregate level; pins the reserve-currency dimension of the FWTW matrix. It is a global/group-level marginal only, not a from-whom-to-whom cell: it has no creditor-by-debtor bilateral detail and no individual reporting-country detail (individual-country reserve composition is confidential).

Bilateral portfolio investment positions (equity + debt securities) between economies by counterpart economy and by holder/issuer sector; supplies the portfolio-investment counterparty (from-whom-to-whom) layer of the global SFC matrix.

contracts.publisher: OECD (Statistics and Data Directorate, National Accounts — agency OECD.SDD.NAD), served via the OECD SDMX API at sdmx.oecd.org
contracts.role_in_matrix: Supplies non-US / non-euro-area advanced-economy sectoral financial balance sheets (stock marginals by institutional sector x instrument) and, where the counterpart-information dataflows are populated, from-whom-to-whom (counterparty-sector) cells. Primary marginal source confirmed to cover JPN, GBR, CAN, CHE, KOR, NOR, SWE, DNK, ISL, ISR, NZL, AUS-region peers etc. via the non-consolidated balance-sheet dataflow.

contracts.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury (with the Federal Reserve Board) — Treasury International Capital (TIC) reporting system
contracts.role_in_matrix: Supplies the bilateral dollar-asset layer of the FWTW matrix: foreign holdings of U.S. securities (U.S. liabilities) and U.S. holdings of foreign securities (U.S. claims), broken down by counterparty country, plus monthly Major Foreign Holders (MFH) of U.S. Treasuries by country. These provide the from-whom-to-whom cells for cross-border holdings of dollar (and U.S.-resident) securities.

Official-sector gold holdings by country, providing the non-dollar reserve-asset (monetary gold) dimension of central-bank/official-sector reserves; complements IMF COFER currency-composition reserves in the FWTW matrix.
